channel.title: NYT > Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How Elon Musk Used SpaceX to Benefit Himself and His Businesses
item.description: The rocket maker has been a useful financial tool for Mr. Musk, providing the billionaire with loans and aiding his struggling companies, a Times examination found.
item.dc:creator: Susanne Craig and Kirsten Grind

item.title: Sam Altman’s Next High-Wire Act: Getting OpenAI to Make More Money
item.description: Mr. Altman, who has faced criticism over OpenAI’s direction, has culled company projects and is trying to be more disciplined with strategy.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Javier Palma; Source Photographs by Rod Lamkey Jr. for The New York Times; Karsten Moran for The New York Times; Getty

item.title: 5 Tall Tasks for John Ternus, Apple’s Next C.E.O.
item.description: John Ternus will face many of the same issues that Tim Cook has grappled with for years.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang
item.media:description: John Ternus, Apple’s next chief executive, is a company veteran who ran Apple’s hardware engineering.

item.title: After Correspondents Dinner Shooting, Rumors and Conspiracy Theories Swirl Online
item.description: Influencers jumped to fill the information void with conspiracy theories about the attack at the White House Correspondents’ dinner on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson, Tiffany Hsu and Steven Lee Myers
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Security outside the Washington Hilton after gun shots were fired during the 2026 White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner Saturday night.

item.title: Social Media Captures the Chaos and Mundane of Shooting at Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: Journalists and guests shared the surreal and confusing scenes from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner after gunfire erupted on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: The 2026 White House Correspondents’ Association dinner at the Washington Hilton after gunfire on Saturday night.

item.title: A Year Later, Trump’s ‘Most Exclusive’ Memecoin Event Is a Lot Less Exclusive
item.description: Even a Times reporter qualified for the event, which caused outrage last year for providing access to President Trump in exchange for investment in one of his family’s crypto ventures.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany

item.title: Thousands of EV Car Leases Are Ending Soon, Giving Buyers a More Affordable Option
item.description: The leases on hundreds of thousands of battery-powered cars and trucks will end in the next three years, and many will end up on used-car lots.
item.dc:creator: Neal E. Boudette
item.media:description: Analysts say lower prices of used electric cars and the surging cost of gasoline will encourage more people to consider models that they may have found too expensive earlier.

item.title: Google Commits to Invest Up to $40 Billion in Anthropic
item.description: The investment comes as the A.I. start-up looks to keep up with accelerating demand for its business and coding products.
item.dc:creator: Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: Google investment in Anthropic tightens the companies’ relationship even as they compete in some areas.

item.title: What Are Prediction Markets, and Why Are They Causing Controversy?
item.description: The indictment of a soldier who bet on the U.S. operation to capture President Nicolás Maduro of Venezuela put renewed focus on a new way to gamble, and a new way to cheat.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:description: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are a growing phenomenon.

item.title: Tim Cook’s Legacy + The Future of U.B.I. With Andrew Yang + HatGPT
item.description: The highlights, the lowlights, and how Apple changed in the years since Tim Cook took over as chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Rachel Cohn, Whitney Jones, Vjeran Pavic, Alyssa Moxley, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano and Rowan Niemisto
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo: Doug Mills/The New York Times

item.title: A.I. Start-Ups From Canada and Germany Merge to Take On Silicon Valley
item.description: Cohere is acquiring Aleph Alpha in a deal aimed at customers uneasy about the dominance of American companies in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano
item.media:credit: Brendan Ko for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nick Frosst, left, and Ivan Zhang, co-founders of Cohere, in 2022. The start-up was seen as one of the few companies to challenge its Silicon Valley rivals.

item.title: Intel’s Revenues Soar, Aided by A.I. Boom
item.description: The chip maker reported a 7 percent rise to $13.6 billion in its latest quarter, more than $1 billion more than Wall Street expected.
item.dc:creator: Don Clark
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Intel’s manufacturing campus in Ocotillo, Ariz., where a federally subsidized expansion is underway.

item.title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
item.description: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac and Eli Tan
item.media:description: Meta’s headquarters in Menlo Park, Calif.

item.title: OpenAI Unveils Its New, More Powerful GPT-5.5 Model
item.description: The maker of ChatGPT is taking a more open approach to cybersecurity than its chief rival, Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:description: OpenAI said its new technology was better at writing computer code and tasks related to other office work.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s Epic Fight Heads to Court
item.description: A jury trial that is set to start on Monday could shift the course of the A.I. race, with Mr. Musk seeking billions of dollars in damages from Mr. Altman’s OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mr. Musk in February with Shivon Zilis, the mother of four of his children and a former OpenAI board member. She is expected to testify at the trial.

item.title: New Phishing Scam: Fake Invitations
item.description: Hackers are spoofing Paperless Post, Evite and Punchbowl to creep into your hard drive.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Jack Nisberg/Condé Nast, via Getty Images
item.media:description: That party you were invited to? It may not be real.

item.title: Crypto Entrepreneur Justin Sun Sues Trump Family’s World Liberty Financial, Accusing It of Fraud
item.description: Justin Sun, one of the largest backers of World Liberty Financial, sued the Trump family’s crypto start-up, accusing it of a fraudulent scheme to pressure him into buying digital coins.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany
item.media:credit: Katarina Premfors for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justin Sun, a crypto entrepreneur, at a conference last year in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Mythos A.I. Model Sets Off Global Alarms
item.description: Mythos has triggered emergency responses from central banks and intelligence agencies globally, as Anthropic decides who has access to the powerful model.
item.dc:creator: Paul Mozur and Adam Satariano

item.title: Leaked Code for Anthropic’s Claude Code Tests Copyright Challenges in A.I. Era
item.description: Artificial intelligence tools are making it faster than ever to reproduce creative work. Does copyright even matter anymore?
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.media:credit: Grant Harder for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sigrid Jin, a student at the University of British Columbia in Vancouver, Canada, used A.I. agents to rewrite leaked Anthropic code.

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Goals Shift Ahead of Its I.P.O.
item.description: As SpaceX prepares to go public, Mr. Musk has proposed moonshots that differ from the company’s original aim of reaching Mars.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac

item.title: Aging in Place: How Technology Might Help You Grow Old at Home
item.description: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Susan Shain

item.title: A Wish List for John Ternus, the Man Replacing Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
item.description: John Ternus, an Apple veteran who runs hardware engineering, will take over an extraordinarily profitable company in need of new ideas.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen
item.media:description: John Ternus, shown in 2018, will inherit a wildly successful company in search of new ideas.

item.title: Amazon Plans to Invest Up to $25 Billion in Anthropic
item.description: Anthropic also committed to spending $100 billion on Amazon technologies that can help build and deploy A.I. systems.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Karen Weise
item.media:credit: Spencer Lowell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Over the past year, Anthropic has become one of the largest users of Amazon’s latest chip, known as Trainium.

item.title: Apple C.E.O.s Through the Years: From Michael Scott (Not That One) to John Ternus
item.description: Apple’s corner office has been a seat from which executives like Steve Jobs changed how we interact with technology.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: The first Apple Macintosh computer in 1984.

item.title: Tim Cook Will Step Down as Apple C.E.O.
item.description: The longtime leader of the iPhone maker will be replaced by John Ternus, the company’s head of hardware engineering.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle
item.media:description: Tim Cook joined Apple in 1998 and was its chief operating officer under the company’s co-founder Steve Jobs.

item.title: Elon Musk Ignores French Prosecutors, Widening Tech Rift With Europe
item.description: Prosecutors investigating his social media company, X, had summoned him for a meeting. His no-show reflected a broader dispute over regulation.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.media:description: Elon Musk speaking in Switzerland in January. He has previously denounced the French investigation.

item.title: California Accuses Amazon of Price Fixing in Legal Filing
item.description: The state claimed the e-commerce giant pressured brands like Levi’s and Hanes to ask competing retailers to raise prices on certain products.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The lawsuit California brought against Amazon is scheduled to go to trial next year.
